_status: scaffold — LoRA not yet trained; rubric not yet authored at full rigor. Production-ready target: week 2-3 of catalog buildout.

origin.region: Warli/Varli Adivasi community, North Sahyadri Range, Maharashtra (also Gujarat, Daman & Diu)
origin.antiquity: Documented since ~10th century CE; ritual wall-painting tradition tied to harvest, marriage, and Tarpa dance ceremonies
origin.primary_practitioners: Adivasi (tribal) men and women of the Warli community; modern recognition includes Jivya Soma Mashe (Padma Shri 2011), Madhukar Vadu, and the Warli Adivasi Mahasabha
origin.geocultural_landscape: Originated as wall paintings on mud-plaster (terracotta-red ground), drawn in rice-paste white. Sacred origin in Palghat-region ritual practice.

visual_signature.line_discipline: Single-thickness geometric primitive lines; circles for heads and torsos, triangles for bodies, straight lines for limbs
visual_signature.color_strategy: Traditionally TWO COLORS ONLY — white rice-paste on terracotta/mud-red ground. Contemporary Warli on paper uses similar restraint: white-on-brown OR brown-on-white.
visual_signature.composition: Crowded scenes — many small figures arranged in scenes (harvest, dance, hunting); minimal hierarchy; trees and animals integrated with humans
visual_signature.ornament_density: Minimal — the geometric primitives ARE the aesthetic; no interior decoration of figures
visual_signature.background_strategy: Filled with secondary motifs (sun, moon, animals, smaller figures, trees); never empty
visual_signature.negative_space_treatment: Composition tightly populated; minimal blank space but no obligation to fill every pixel like Madhubani
visual_signature.ground_line: Implied by scene composition (humans on ground line); no decorative ground-mark required

border_grammars: minimal — borders are uncommon in Warli; framing is by composition, not by decorative border

prompt_scaffold.positive_prefix: Warli folk-art painting of {subject}, single-thickness white lines on terracotta-mud background, geometric primitives — circles for heads, triangles for bodies, straight lines for limbs, minimalist tribal Adivasi aesthetic from North Sahyadri Maharashtra, no interior decoration of figures, traditional two-color Warli grammar
prompt_scaffold.negative_prefix: photorealistic, 3D render, photographic shading, perspective depth, anatomically accurate proportions, multi-color, ornate decoration, Madhubani-style interior fill, vector clean lines, Disney style, anime style, watercolor, oil paint
hard_negatives_v0_1: no multi-color body fill — Warli is strictly two-color (white + terracotta)
no interior body decoration — bodies are SOLID line geometry, never filled with ornament
no anatomically realistic proportions — figures are GEOMETRIC PRIMITIVES (triangle bodies, circle heads)
no vector-perfect lines — single-thickness but hand-drawn quality
no realistic animal anatomy — animals are stylized to geometric primitives too

production_recipe.caption_template: a warli folk-art painting depicting [SUBJECT], traditional adivasi tribal style from north sahyadri maharashtra, white lines on terracotta mud, geometric primitives

lora_training.reference_corpus_status: TO COLLECT: ~50 attributable Warli paintings from Wikimedia, Jivya Soma Mashe archive, Warli Adivasi Mahasabha public collection, V&A Museum

qc_rubric._status: to_be_authored — Madhubani.v9 rubric NOT directly applicable. Warli requires its own checks because the grammar (geometric primitives, two-color, no interior decoration) is the OPPOSITE of Madhubani's ornament-rich style.
planned_checks: geometric-primitive-discipline (figures composed of circles/triangles/lines, not curves)
no-interior-fill (bodies are line geometry, not color-filled)
composition-density (multiple figures or scene context, not single isolated subject)
anatomy-as-stylization (recognizable species via geometric abstraction, not realism)

icon_64.notes: Warli's geometric primitives survive 64×64 better than any other tradition — the icon king

gallery_a1.notes: Vectorize first, then rasterize at A1 — pristine at any size

vector_svg.notes: Warli is the most vector-friendly tradition; SVG export via potrace is a first-class output

cultural_attribution.tradition_owners: Warli (Varli) Adivasi community, North Sahyadri Range, Maharashtra, India
cultural_attribution.mandatory_attribution_text: This Warli-tradition artifact draws on the folk-art lineage of the Warli Adivasi community of Maharashtra, India. Originated and sustained by Adivasi artists; brought to international recognition through Jivya Soma Mashe (Padma Shri, 2011) and the Warli Adivasi Mahasabha.
cultural_attribution.commercial_use_note: Commercial use should support Warli artist cooperatives, the Warli Adivasi Mahasabha, and Adivasi cultural-preservation organizations. The tradition is community-owned, not individually owned — attribution to the community is mandatory; attribution to any single artist requires their direct consent.

novelty_handling.policy: Same as Madhubani — subjects newly introduced to Warli (snow leopards, dugongs, animals from non-Maharashtrian regions) are explicitly disclosed as contemporary interpolations in receipts.
novelty_handling.additional_warli_specific_note: Warli has historically depicted local fauna (tiger, deer, peacock, fish, bullock). Extending it to global fauna or to non-Maharashtrian Indian species (snow leopard, hoolock gibbon, etc.) requires explicit novelty disclosure.
